steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The team concludes previous assessments underestimated particle capacity. We need the choice that explains why prior studies came up short — specifically why they measured less than the true amount.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'prior studies used extraction methods that don't affect wax': The experiment shows alkaline extraction (which removes wax) recovered 53.8%, while water recovered only 7.6%. If prior methods couldn't dissolve wax, they would have missed the particles trapped there — directly explaining the underestimation. ✓
  • Choice B — 'tree species diversity': Tree diversity is unrelated to the extraction-method gap the results reveal. ✗
  • Choice C — 'concentrations exceeding 53.8%': This contradicts the data; 53.8% is the highest recovery shown. ✗
  • Choice D — 'treatment order': The order of treatments doesn't explain why wax-bound particles were missed. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
